description: A post supports a roof. Evidence may support a claim, and water supports life around a pond. 'panoru' provides what something depends on to stand or continue. 'naphe' (help) enters a particular activity as assistance; support can be the condition underneath it.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into open central unrounded 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let air pass through the nose into stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r', then raise the back of the tongue and round the lips for high-back 'u'.
usage_notes: Whatever receives support is the direct object. The event-noun rule supplies support. Evidence can support a claim without settling whether the claim is true or the inference sound.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared infrastructure can be tested by what it actually supports. A system called public can still leave people or ecological relations without what they need.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Support makes interdependence practical: a person or practice continues through conditions that no act of will supplies alone.

semantic_domains.activity: providing what another activity or condition depends on
semantic_domains.cognition: evidence or reasoning that supports a claim
semantic_domains.community: practical or social support for people and groups
semantic_domains.nature: relations that allow organisms or ecological processes to continue
semantic_domains.physical: bearing weight or holding a structure in place
